key_insight: A regularized Navier–Stokes formulation makes a posteriori error estimation possible even when Dirichlet data is only in L2 and the solution has limited smoothness.
engineering_relevance: This gives a rigorous error indicator for adaptive finite element simulation of incompressible flows with low-regularity boundary conditions, improving reliability of mesh refinement.
method: Introduce a regularized, well-posed approximation of the stationary Navier–Stokes problem.
Discretize with conforming Taylor–Hood P2/P1 finite elements.
Build a residual-based a posteriori estimator and prove reliability and efficiency under small-data assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports computable upper and lower bounds that link the estimator to the true error between the exact solution and the finite element approximation.
watch_item: The reliability and efficiency results depend on suitable smallness assumptions on the data, so the method may be restricted outside that regime.
paper_title: A posteriori error analysis for the Navier-Stokes equations with non-smooth data
